title: Weak Fault Isolation
summary: Failures in one component easily spread to others, causing cascading outages.
inspection_goal: Inspect whether the dependency graph amplifies failures instead of containing them.
primary_signals: Many critical paths converge on the same component
Long dependency chains without buffering boundaries
Bridge nodes whose failure would disconnect large regions
Tight synchronous cycles across critical components
inspection_questions: If this node fails, how much of the graph becomes degraded?
Which dependencies create large blast radii?
Where is there no structural buffering or containment?
Are core paths too synchronous and tightly chained?
evidence_to_collect: Cycle and SCC results on critical components
recommendation_patterns: Introduce isolation boundaries and fallback behavior
Decouple critical paths with queues or retries where appropriate
Make dependencies optional or degradable where possible

severity_guidance.high: A small set of failures could structurally impact large parts of the system.
severity_guidance.medium: Some dependency paths have poor containment but blast radius is still limited.
